title: carthamus × battandieri
intro: Carthamus × battandieri, also known as the safflower or false safflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Carthamus × battandieri include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting heart health and lowering cholesterol levels. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and topical creams, and its seeds are often used as a substitute for safflower oil, which is rich in omega-6 fatty acids.
